text:
Admission Date:  2011-12-28              Discharge Date:   1998-01-11


Service: NEUROSURGERY

Allergies:
metformin / Sulfa(Sulfonamide Antibiotics)

Attending:Benjamin
Chief Complaint:
HA s/p fall


Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
None


History of Present Illness:
[**Age over 90 **] F with history of coumadin use (unknown reason) with fall
at home last night.  Denies LOC.  Fall was mechanical-denies
preceding CP, SOB, palpitations.  R head strike.  Currently with
R pain.


Past Medical History:
HTN, DM


Social History:
NC

Family History:
NC

Physical Exam:
O: T: 99  BP: 162/64         HR: 83   R 22      O2Sats 99%
Gen: WD/WN, comfortable, NAD.
HEENT: Pupils: 05-28         EOMs intact
Neck: Supple.
Lungs: CTA bilaterally.
Cardiac: RRR. S1/S2.
Abd: Soft, NT, BS+
Extrem: Warm and well-perfused.
Neuro:
Mental status: Awake and alert, cooperative with exam, normal
affect.
Orientation: Oriented to person, place, and date.
Recall: 04-27 objects at 5 minutes.
Language: Speech fluent with good comprehension and repetition.
Naming intact. No dysarthria or paraphasic errors.

Cranial Nerves:
I: Not tested
II: Pupils equally round and reactive to light, to
mm bilaterally. Visual fields are full to confrontation.
III, IV, VI: Extraocular movements intact bilaterally without
nystagmus.
V, VII: Facial strength and sensation intact and symmetric.
VIII: Hearing intact to voice.
IX, X: Palatal elevation symmetrical.
Victoria: Sternocleidomastoid and trapezius normal bilaterally.
XII: Tongue midline without fasciculations.

Motor: Normal bulk and tone bilaterally. No abnormal movements,
tremors. Strength full power 10-08 throughout. No pronator drift.
Bilateral hip flexion limited 02-19 arthritis

Sensation: Intact to light touch bilaterally.

Toes downgoing bilaterally

On Discharge:
A&Ox3
PERRL
EOMs intact
Face symmetrical
tongue midline
Motor: full


Pertinent Results:
CT HEAD W/O CONTRAST 2010-11-13
1. Small subdural hematoma along the right frontoparietal
convexity, with
minimal mass effect on subjacent brain, unchanged over the
approximately 17 hour interval.
2. No new hemorrhage


Brief Hospital Course:
[**Age over 90 **] y/o F s/p fall on coumadin presents with R SDH. Patient was
neurologically stable and was admitted to neurosurgery for
monitoring. It was unclear to patient why she was on coumadin,
but it was held to prevent further bleeding. Upon admission, her
INR was 1.7 and was down to 1.4 on 02-06. She was admitted to the
ICU for Q1H neuro exams and started on dilantin to prevent
seizures. On 04-26, patient was stable on exam. She was found to
have a UTI and was started on cipro. The patient expressed that
she currently lives alone and ambulates with a walker, PT was
consulted for evaluation and home safety. She was transferred to
the floor. On 01-11, it was determined that the patient was safe
to discharge home with 24 hr supervision. She was in stable
condition at time of discharge.

Medications on Admission:
metoprolol, glipizide,
lisinopril, omeprazole, coumadin, zocor


Discharge Medications:
1. Ciprofloxacin HCl 250 mg PO Q12H
RX *ciprofloxacin 250 mg 1 tablet(s) by mouth every twelve (12)
hours Disp #*6 Tablet Refills:*0
2. Phenytoin Sodium Extended 100 mg PO Q8H
RX *phenytoin sodium extended 100 mg 1 capsule(s) by mouth three
times a day Disp #*60 Capsule Refills:*2


Discharge Disposition:
Home With Service

Facility:
Specialty Hospital Jacksonville Home Care

Discharge Diagnosis:
R SDH


Discharge Condition:
Mental Status: Clear and coherent.
Level of Consciousness: Alert and interactive.
Activity Status: Ambulatory - requires assistance or aid (walker
or cane).


Discharge Instructions:
??????	Take your pain medicine as prescribed if needed. You do not
need to take it if you do not have pain.
??????	Exercise should be limited to walking; no lifting, straining,
or excessive bending.
??????	Increase your intake of fluids and fiber, as narcotic pain
medicine can cause constipation. We generally recommend taking
an over the counter stool softener, such as Docusate (Colace)
while taking narcotic pain medication.
??????	DO not take any anti-inflammatory medicines such as Motrin,
Aspirin, Advil, or Ibuprofen etc. until follow up.
??????       You should follow up with your primary care physician
regarding need for coumadin. You are not prescribed coumadin at
this time. We do not recommend that you take coumadin until seen
in follow up with your primary care physician and [**Name9 (PRE) 112531**].
??????	***You have been prescribed Dilantin (Phenytoin), take it as
prescribed and follow up with laboratory blood drawing in one
week.  This can be drawn at your PCP??????s office, but please have
the results faxed to 21-823-1729.
??????	Do not drive until your follow up appointment.

CALL YOUR SURGEON IMMEDIATELY IF YOU EXPERIENCE ANY OF THE
FOLLOWING

??????	New onset of tremors or seizures.
??????	Any confusion, lethargy or change in mental status.
??????	Any numbness, tingling, weakness in your extremities.
??????	Pain or headache that is continually increasing, or not
relieved by pain medication.
??????	New onset of the loss of function, or decrease of function on
one whole side of your body.


Followup Instructions:
Follow-Up Appointment Instructions

??????Please call (425-381-9279 to schedule an appointment with Dr.
Cole, to be seen in 4 weeks.
??????You will need a CT scan of the brain without contrast prior to
your appointment. This can be scheduled when you call to make
your office visit appointment.
??????We recommend you see Dr Sandra in the Traumatic Brain Injury
(TBI) clinic the phone number is 123-638-3042. If you have any
problems booking this appointment please ask for Ryan Morrison.


                             April Rich Trevor Mcmahon MD 12791

Completed by:1998-01-11
